newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. The last major item is pre-window: TSMC's May 15 announcement to sell 152M VIS shares (8.1%, ~US$850M block trade), cutting its stake from ~27.1% to ~19% while explicitly preserving interposer outsourcing and GaN technology licensing to VIS. That overhang has been absorbed (stock sits ~13% below its May 7 all-time high of TWD 185.5). June 9's +2.9% move is below the 5% threshold and looks flow-driven Taiwan-semis beta ahead of the ~June 10 May monthly revenue release.

sources.label: TSMC press release - sale of 8.1% of VIS
TrendForce - TSMC plans to sell 8.1% stake in Vanguard (May 15, 2026)
Investing.com - VIS shares slide after TSMC trims stake

verdicts.rankRationale: On the manufacturing-tollgate lens nothing changed since June 6: VIS keeps TSMC interposer outsourcing, GaN licensing, and the VSMC 12-inch Singapore JV (first output 2027), and a +2.9% no-news day does not alter node positioning. Yesterday's hold stands.
verdicts.bull: Structural interposer outsourcing from TSMC plus GaN licensing makes VIS a quiet capacity relief valve for advanced packaging, with VSMC 12-inch capacity layering on from 2027.
verdicts.neutral: Specialty 8-inch foundry economics (PMIC, drivers) dominate the P&L today; the AI-adjacent interposer/photonics story is real but still a small revenue slice.
verdicts.bear: Mature-node pricing pressure from Chinese foundries and a slow VSMC ramp could leave VIS a commodity specialty foundry trading at an AI-narrative multiple.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May monthly revenue (~June 10) and any quantification of interposer/advanced-packaging volumes at the Aug 4 results.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Mature-node price war re-intensifying and diluting the tollgate premium.

verdicts.rankRationale: Alpha lens: +74% YTD with no new fundamental evidence since June 6 means residual alpha keeps compressing, but the stock is already ~13% off its May 7 high, the TSMC block overhang cleared pre-window, and the page already dropped it from 19 to 32 - today's +2.9% drift does not justify another move beyond yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: TSMC selling down to 19% while contractually retaining interposer outsourcing improves float and institutional access just as advanced-packaging demand outruns capacity; the post-block pullback restored some asymmetry.
verdicts.neutral: The name has rerated from hidden specialty foundry to recognized AI-packaging derivative; at ~$11B cap and 74% YTD, remaining upside depends on interposer/photonics revenue actually showing up, not on discovery.
verdicts.bear: This is now a consensus AI-packaging proxy; any soft monthly revenue print or VSMC capex/ramp slippage hits a stock priced for flawless execution, and TSMC's sale caps the strategic-takeout angle.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May/June monthly revenue confirming the PMIC-plus-interposer recovery, or a named advanced-packaging capacity expansion.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: a flow-driven Taiwan-semis pullback hits recent reraters like VIS hardest absent new evidence.
